result.markdown:
--- title: "Analysis: Dog Poop Complaints in Manhattan" format: html: code-fold: true --- ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Use quarto render to generate a static site in /docs # Set github pages to deploy from main branch, /docs library(flextable) library(sf) library(tidyverse) library(socratadata) library(lubridate) library(mapgl) library(glue) library(scales) # If needed, ensure that the Quarto render and RStudio read the same environment vars readRenviron(".Renviron") ``` ::: New York City's 311 program has six service request categories related to dog poop. They are: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} dog_poop_sr <- read_csv("dog_poop_SRs.csv") dog_poop_sr |> flextable() |> set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit") |> align(j = 1, align = "left", part = "all") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Agency

Complaint Type

Descriptor

Additional_Details

Location_Type

Department of Sanitation

Dirty Condition

Not Cleaned by Property Owner

Yes

Department of Sanitation

Yes

Sidewalk

Department of Sanitation

Dirty Condition

Dog Waste

Animal in a Park

Animal Waste

Dog

Street/Curbside

``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} dog_poop_complaints <- soc_read( "https://data.cityofnewyork.us/Social-Services/311-Service-Requests-from-2020-to-Present/erm2-nwe9/about_data", query = soc_query( where = "borough = 'MANHATTAN' AND ((descriptor = 'Dog Waste') OR (descriptor = 'Animal Waste' AND descriptor_2 = 'Dog'))" ) ) min_date <- min(as_date(dog_poop_complaints$created_date)) max_date <- max(as_date(dog_poop_complaints$created_date)) n_complaints <- nrow(dog_poop_complaints) ``` ::: Based on these six service request types, there were **2,515** dog poop-related service requests filed in Manhattan between **2020-01-03** and **2026-04-06**. A clear annual trend is visible when aggregating these service requests to year and month: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} monthly_dog_poop_complaints <- dog_poop_complaints |> st_drop_geometry() |> group_by(yearMonth = paste0( year(created_date), "-", str_split_i(date(created_date), "-", 2))) |> summarize(monthly_complaints = n()) |> ungroup() |> # Pin to first of month for plotting mutate(date = as.Date(paste0(yearMonth, "-01"))) ggplot(monthly_dog_poop_complaints, aes(x = date, y = monthly_complaints)) + geom_point()+ scale_x_date( date_breaks = "1 year", date_minor_breaks = "1 month", date_labels = "%Y" ) + labs(x = "Date (Year, Month)", y = "Dog Poop Service Requests") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-1.svg){width=100%} ::: ::: Since 2022, dog poop-related 311 service requests have tended to be highest in January, February, and March, with a decreasing trend throughout the calendar year. 2024 is an exception to this trend; the count of service requests increased throughout the calendar year. Based on the peak winter months in 2022, 2023, and 2025, the overall volume of dog-poop service requests in Manhattan appears to be increasing. The back-to-back snowstorms in February of 2026 appear to have led to approximately three times the number of dog poop related service requests than in February of 2025. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} dog_poop_complaints_2026 <- dog_poop_complaints |> filter(year(created_date)>2025) dog_poop_complaints_2026_by_cb <- dog_poop_complaints |> st_drop_geometry() |> filter(year(created_date)>2025) |> group_by(community_board) |> summarize(complaints_2026 = n()) ``` ::: In 2026 alone, there have been **452** dog poop related service requests. The spatial distribution of these complaints is not homogenous: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} dog_poop_complaints_2026_by_cb |> flextable() |> set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit") |> align(j = 1, align = "left", part = "all") |> set_header_labels( community_board = "Community Board", complaints_2026 = "Dog Poop Service Requests" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Community Board

Dog Poop Service Requests

01 MANHATTAN

8

2

22

152

Unspecified MANHATTAN

15

``` ::: ::: In 2026, the greatest number of dog poop related service requests have come from Manhattan Community Districts 10 and 12. Interestingly, a few Community Districts (1, 2, 5) have had very few in comparison. Visually: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} suppressMessages( comDist <- read_sf("https://services5.arcgis.com/GfwWNkhOj9bNBqoJ/arcgis/rest/services/NYC_Community_Districts/FeatureServer/0/query?where=1=1&outFields=*&outSR=4326&f=pgeojson") |> st_make_valid() |> filter(BoroCD < 200) |> mutate(community_board = paste0( str_sub(as.character(BoroCD), -2), " MANHATTAN" )) |> left_join(dog_poop_complaints_2026_by_cb) |> mutate( tooltip_html = glue( "Community Board: {community_board}
", "Service requests: {complaints_2026}" ) ) ) maplibre(style = carto_style("positron")) |> fit_bounds(comDist) |> add_fill_layer( id = "Community Districts", source = comDist, fill_color = interpolate( column = "complaints_2026", values = c( min(comDist$complaints_2026), max(comDist$complaints_2026) ), stops = c("#fff5f0", "#ef3b2c") ), fill_opacity = 0.7, tooltip = "tooltip_html" ) |> add_circle_layer( id = "Service Requests", source = dog_poop_complaints_2026, circle_color = "#7f7f7f", circle_stroke_color = "#cccccc", circle_radius = 5, circle_stroke_width = 1 ) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Because the service requests are associated with a specific location given by latitude and longitude coordinates, they may be aggregated to different areas. For example, a public awareness campaign may utilize LinkNYC Kiosks with screens. The number of 2026 service requests within a five minute (~ 0.25mi / 400m) walk of these locations may be determined: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} mn_link_withScreens <- soc_read( "https://data.cityofnewyork.us/Social-Services/LinkNYC-Kiosk-Locations/s4kf-3yrf/about_data", query = soc_query( where = "planned_kiosk_type != 'LINK5G_NoAd' AND link_installation_status != 'Removed Pending Reinstall' AND boro = 'Manhattan'" ) ) |> st_as_sf(coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326) mn_link_withScreens$sr_count <- st_intersects(mn_link_withScreens |> st_buffer(dist = 400), dog_poop_complaints_2026) |> lengths() mn_link_withScreens |> st_drop_geometry() |> group_by(sr_count) |> summarize(n_kiosks = n()) |> ungroup() |> ggplot(aes(x = sr_count, y = n_kiosks)) + geom_point() + labs(x = "Number of Dog Poop Service Requests", y = "Number of LinkNYC Kiosks within a Five-Minute Walk") + scale_x_continuous(minor_breaks = seq(0,60,5)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-1.svg){width=100%} ::: ::: Of the **1,225** LinkNYC kiosks in Manhattan with a screen, **371** do not have a dog poop service request in 2026 within a five minute walk. **378** kiosks have five or more service requests within this area. **92** kiosks have 10 or more service requests within this area. Let's take a look at the relationship between dog licenses and dog poop service requests. The dog license dataset is reported at the Zip Code level, so we'll aggregate 2026 service requests to that level: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} manhattan_zcta <- read_sf("https://services2.arcgis.com/FiaPA4ga0iQKduv3/ArcGIS/rest/services/Census_ZIP_Code_Tabulation_Areas_2010_v1/FeatureServer/0/query?where=1%3D1&geometry=-74.0479%2C40.6829%2C-73.9067%2C40.8820&geometryType=esriGeometryEnvelope&inSR=4326&spatialRel=esriSpatialRelIntersects&outFields=*&outSR=4326&f=geojson")|> filter(str_starts(ZCTA5, "10") & !str_starts(ZCTA5, "104")) dog_licenses <- soc_read( "https://data.cityofnewyork.us/Health/NYC-Dog-Licensing-Dataset/nu7n-tubp/about_data", query = soc_query( where = "zipcode < 10400" ) ) dog_licenses_by_zip <- dog_licenses |> group_by(zipcode) |> summarize(n_dog_licenses = n()) |> mutate(zipcode = as.character(zipcode)) manhattan_zcta$dog_poop_SRs_26 <- st_intersects(manhattan_zcta, dog_poop_complaints_2026) |> lengths() manhattan_zcta <- manhattan_zcta |> left_join(dog_licenses_by_zip, by = join_by("ZCTA5" == "zipcode")) ggplot(manhattan_zcta |> filter(!(dog_poop_SRs_26 == 0 & is.na(n_dog_licenses))), aes(x = n_dog_licenses, y = dog_poop_SRs_26)) + geom_point() + geom_vline(aes(xintercept = median(n_dog_licenses, na.rm = T)), linetype = 2, color = "grey40") + geom_hline(aes(yintercept = median(dog_poop_SRs_26, na.rm = T)), linetype = 2, color = "grey40")+ scale_x_continuous(label = comma) + labs(x = "Dog Licenses", y = "2026 YTD Dog Poop Service Requests") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-9-1.svg){width=100%} ::: ::: Above, the dotted lines indicate the median for each axis' variable, separating the zip codes into Low - Low, Low - High, High - Low, and High - High quadrants. Turning to 311 Service Requests related to rodent activity, the various specific complaint types can be queried using the following five conditions: - Agency = Department of Education & Descriptor = Rodents/Mice - Agency = Department of Health and Mental Hygiene & descriptor = Rodents/Insects/Garbage - Agency = Department of Health and Mental Hygiene & Complaint Type = Rodent - Agency = Department of Parks and Recreation & Descriptor = Rodent Sighting - Agency = Department of Sanitation & Descriptor = Insects or Rodents ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} rodent_SRs <- soc_read( "https://data.cityofnewyork.us/Social-Services/311-Service-Requests-from-2020-to-Present/erm2-nwe9/about_data", query = soc_query( where = "borough = 'MANHATTAN' AND ( (agency_name = 'Department of Education' AND descriptor = 'Rodents/Mice') OR (agency_name = 'Department of Health and Mental Hygiene' AND descriptor = 'Rodents/Insects/Garbage') OR (agency_name = 'Department of Health and Mental Hygiene' AND complaint_type = 'Rodent') OR (agency_name = 'Department of Parks and Recreation' AND descriptor = 'Rodent Sighting') OR (agency_name = 'Department of Sanitation' AND descriptor = 'Insects or Rodents'))" ) ) rodent_SRs_2026 <- rodent_SRs |> filter(year(created_date)>2025) manhattan_zcta$rodent_SRs_26 <- st_intersects(manhattan_zcta, rodent_SRs_2026) |> lengths() ``` ::: There have been **1,826** rodent-related 311 Service Requests in Manhattan in 2026 to date. Again, aggregating to Zip Code: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot(manhattan_zcta |> filter(!(dog_poop_SRs_26 == 0 & is.na(n_dog_licenses))), aes(x = dog_poop_SRs_26, y = rodent_SRs_26)) + geom_point() + geom_vline(aes(xintercept = median(dog_poop_SRs_26, na.rm = T)), linetype = 2, color = "grey40") + geom_hline(aes(yintercept = median(rodent_SRs_26, na.rm = T)), linetype = 2, color = "grey40") + labs(x = "2026 YTD Dog Poop Service Requests", y = "2026 YTD Rodent Service Requests") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-11-1.svg){width=100%} ::: ::: As above, the variable medians separate Manhattan Zip Codes into quadrants. Within the High - High quadrant, dog poop service requests and rodent-related service requests are positively correlated.
